html:
[402]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 妖巫 - Refers to a witch or sorceress, often associated with supernatural powers and practices.
  • 致祸 - Means 'to bring disaster' or 'to cause misfortune.'
  • 距跃勃跳 - Describes a vigorous or frenzied jumping, often used to depict a person in a state of agitation or excitement.
  • 打筋斗 - A term for somersaulting or performing acrobatic jumps, often used in the context of witchcraft to demonstrate supernatural abilities.
  • 病疮豆 - Refers to skin diseases or rashes, often associated with witchcraft and curses.
  • 畏惮 - Means 'to fear' or 'to dread.'
  • 佐佑 - Means 'to assist' or 'to support,' often used in the context of helping someone in their endeavors.
  • 朝天门 - Refers to a specific place or establishment, possibly a temple or a public space.
  • 寛袖绯衫 - Describes a loose-sleeved red robe, often associated with formal or ceremonial attire.
  • 大黄带 - Refers to a wide yellow belt, often worn as part of a formal or ceremonial outfit.
  • 升髙座 - Means 'to ascend to a high seat,' often used to describe a person taking a prominent or authoritative position.
  • 纵谈祸福 - Means 'to freely discuss good and bad fortune,' often used to describe a person making predictions or giving advice.
  • 只承 - Refers to attendants or followers, often used to describe a group of people who support or assist someone.
  • 弓手 - Refers to a bowman or archer, often a local official or guard.
  • 容洗手致敬 - Means 'to wash hands and show respect,' often used to describe a formal act of reverence.
  • 符法 - Refers to magical formulas or incantations, often used in the context of witchcraft.
  • 隂兵 - Refers to invisible or supernatural soldiers, often used in the context of witchcraft or magic.
  • 虚空 - Refers to emptiness or the void, often used in the context of spiritual or mystical experiences.
  • 棰击 - Means 'to beat' or 'to strike,' often used to describe physical punishment.
  • 白知县 - Means 'to report to the county magistrate,' often used to describe a formal act of reporting or informing.
  • 径具状诣县告 - Means 'to directly prepare a complaint and go to the county office to report,' often used to describe a formal act of filing a complaint.
  • 县宰 - Refers to the county magistrate, the local administrative official.
  • 诘其妖惑 - Means 'to interrogate about the use of witchcraft,' often used to describe a formal investigation.
  • 天心正法 - Refers to a specific form of witchcraft or magic, often described as a method of healing or causing harm.
  • 疗人疾病 - Means 'to cure people's illnesses,' often used to describe the healing aspect of witchcraft.
  • 倨傲 - Means 'arrogant' or 'haughty,' often used to describe a person who is proud and disrespectful.
  • 跳过鼓楼 - Means 'to jump over the drum tower,' often used as a test of supernatural ability.
  • 惶怖 - Means 'frightened' or 'terrified,' often used to describe a person who is scared or anxious.
  • 哀祈 - Means 'to plead pitifully,' often used to describe a person who is begging for mercy or help.
  • 囚于狱 - Means 'to be imprisoned in jail,' often used to describe a formal act of incarceration.
  • 躬往鞫讯 - Means 'to personally go and interrogate,' often used to describe a formal act of investigation.
  • 持正一箓 - Refers to holding a talisman or magical scroll, often used to describe a person who is performing magic.
  • 降魔印 - Refers to a magical seal or stamp, often used to describe a person who is performing magic.
  • 中降魔印 - Means 'to have the magical seal in hand,' often used to describe a person who is performing magic.
  • 错愕 - Means 'to be startled' or 'to be shocked,' often used to describe a person who is surprised or confused.
  • 杖之二十 - Means 'to beat with twenty strokes of the stick,' often used to describe a formal act of punishment.
  • 逐出境 - Means 'to expel from the territory,' often used to describe a formal act of banishment.
  • 初巫以创造法院敛民钱几千緍 - Means 'initially, the witch used the creation of a court to collect money from the people,' often used to describe a formal act of extortion.
  • 拘籍入官帑 - Means 'to seize and register the money into the official treasury,' often used to describe a formal act of confiscation.
  • 充月俸 - Means 'to fill the monthly salary,' often used to describe a formal act of allocation.
  • 邑人为之相贺 - Means 'the townspeople celebrated together,' often used to describe a formal act of celebration.
  • 惧罪 - Means 'to fear punishment,' often used to describe a person who is scared of legal consequences.
  • 徙而之他 - Means 'to move to another place,' often used to describe a formal act of relocation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 婺源懐金乡 - Refers to a specific location in Huizhou, Anhui Province, known for its historical and cultural significance.
  • 汪延瑞 - Refers to a specific individual, possibly a local official or influential figure.
  • 张生 - Refers to a specific individual, the witch in question.
  • 朝天门 - Refers to a specific place or establishment, possibly a temple or a public space.
  • 县弓手 - Refers to a specific local official or guard, often responsible for maintaining order.
  • 洪应贤 - Refers to a specific individual, the county magistrate who investigated the case.
  • 正一箓 - Refers to a specific talisman or magical scroll, often associated with Taoist practices.
  • 降魔印 - Refers to a specific magical seal or stamp, often associated with Taoist practices.

[403]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 治汤火咒 - Specific incantation used to treat burns and fire-related injuries.
  • 俚巫 - Local shamans or folk healers.
  • 持咒语 - Recite or chant spells.
  • 蹈汤火 - Metaphor for performing dangerous feats.
  • 行持 - Practice or perform rituals.
  • 北方壬癸禁火法 - Ritual or method associated with the north and water, used to control fire.
  • 龙树王如来 - Buddhist deity, often associated with wisdom and protection.
  • 真武印 - Symbolic seal or talisman used in rituals.
  • 急急如律令 - Common incantation in Chinese folk religion, meaning 'hastily as the law commands.'
  • 娼女冯妍 - Specific individual, a courtesan named Feng Yan.
  • 姿貌出于流辈 - Her appearance surpasses her peers.
  • 善歌舞 - Skilled in singing and dancing.
  • 谢氏女 - Woman from the Xie family.
  • 诣郡陈状 - Go to the prefecture to report.
  • 卖此女时 - When this daughter was sold.
  • 逾约二年 - Two years over the agreed term.
  • 乞取归养 - Request to take her back for raising.
  • 良家子 - Woman from a respectable family.
  • 风尘中 - Life of debauchery or low social status.
  • 郡守张定叟 - Specific official, the prefect Zhang Ding Sou.
  • 虚妄 - False or untrue.
  • 逐使去 - Dismiss them.
  • 恨涕泣而 - Crying bitterly with regret.
  • 迷不识路 - Lost and unable to recognize the way.
  • 眼前防防漠漠 - State of confusion or disorientation.
  • 把手遮我 - As if hands were covering me.
  • 呈伎 - Perform.
  • 迂枉信足 - Walk aimlessly.
  • 堕砌下 - Fall into the courtyard.
  • 失明 - Lose sight.
  • 眸子宛然 - Eyes are still clear.
  • 其盲自若 - Blindness remains unchanged.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 俚巫 - Local shamans or folk healers common in traditional Chinese society.
  • 龙树王如来 - Significant figure in Buddhism, often invoked in protective and healing rituals.
  • 真武印 - Talisman associated with the God of the North, used in rituals for protection and healing.
  • 急急如律令 - Common incantation in Chinese folk religion, often used to invoke divine power.
  • 郡守 - Local administrative official in ancient China, responsible for the prefecture.
  • 良家子 - Term for women from respectable families, distinguishing them from those of lower social status.
  • 风尘中 - Metaphor for a life of debauchery or low social status, often used to describe courtesans or prostitutes.
  • 呈伎 - Performing arts, such as singing and dancing, common among courtesans.

Proper Nouns

  • 治汤火咒 - Specific incantation used to treat burns and fire-related injuries.
  • 俚巫 - Local shamans or folk healers.
  • 元仲弟 - Specific individual, likely a family member or associate of the author.
  • 龙树王如来 - Buddhist deity, often associated with wisdom and protection.
  • 真武印 - Symbolic seal or talisman used in rituals.
  • 急急如律令 - Common incantation in Chinese folk religion.
  • 娼女冯妍 - Specific individual, a courtesan named Feng Yan.
  • 谢氏女 - Woman from the Xie family.
  • 郡守张定叟 - Specific official, the prefect Zhang Ding Sou.
  • 孙鼎臣 - Specific individual, a judge or official.

[404]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 悖母 - Literally 'defying mother,' but in this context, it likely means 'disobeying mother' or 'causing trouble with mother.'
  • 从母索饭 - Means 'asked his mother for food.'
  • 饭在釡中 - Means 'the rice is in the pot.'
  • 可自去取吃 - Means 'you can go and take it yourself.'
  • 视釡则空无所有 - Means 'looked in the pot and found it empty.'
  • 怒而骂曰 - Means 'angrily cursed, saying.'
  • 你不害瞎妄语如此 - Literally 'you're not blind, why would you lie like this?' Here, '害瞎' is a colloquial expression meaning 'be blind' or 'be foolish,' and '妄语' means 'lying' or 'speaking nonsense.'
  • 即出外折薪一枝 - Means 'immediately went outside and broke off a branch of firewood.'
  • 忽跃而上径入眼贯一睛而出 - This is a vivid and somewhat ambiguous description. '跃而上' means 'leapt up,' '径入眼' means 'directly into the eye,' '贯一睛' means 'pierced one eye,' and '而出' means 'and came out.'
  • 登时晕絶 - Means 'immediately fainted.'
  • 累日乃能起 - Means 'took several days to recover.'
  • 今为孙鼎臣担仆自状其过而悔无及矣 - Means 'now he is a porter for Sun Dingchen, and he recounts his past misdeeds, but it's too late to regret them.'
  • 吴廿九 - A personal name, likely a commoner.
  • 绍熙二年春 - Means 'Spring of the second year of Shaoxi,' a specific date in the Song Dynasty.
  • 金陵民 - Refers to a commoner from Jinling (modern-day Nanjing).
  • 将种稻 - Means 'was going to plant rice.'
  • 从其母假所着皁绨袍 - Means 'asked his mother to lend him the black silk robe he was wearing.'
  • 明日插秧要典钱与雇人工食费 - Means 'tomorrow we need money for planting, hiring workers, and food expenses.'
  • 我怕春寒 - Means 'I'm afraid of the spring cold.'
  • 且明日未必成 - Means 'and tomorrow might not work out.'
  • 汝妻自有袄 - Means 'your wife has her own coat.'
  • 何不就取 - Means 'why don't you just take it?'
  • 恚而退 - Means 'angrily left.'
  • 其家有桑十余株 - Means 'his family has over ten mulberry trees.'
  • 妇姑中分之矣 - Means 'the wife and mother-in-law had divided them equally.'
  • 姑误采妇叶 - Means 'the mother-in-law mistakenly picked the wife's mulberry leaves.'
  • 告吴 - Means 'told Wu.'
  • 吴即入母房牵之出 - Means 'Wu immediately went into his mother's room and dragged her out.'
  • 不借我袄 - Means 'you didn't lend me the coat.'
  • 又采我叶 - Means 'and you picked my mulberry leaves.'
  • 莫要在此住自去别处讨饭吃 - Means 'don't live here anymore, go find food somewhere else.'
  • 持斧斫其床并荐席亦碎毁 - Means 'he took an axe and chopped up the bed and the straw mat, destroying them too.'
  • 时已逼暮 - Means 'it was already late in the evening.'
  • 婆儿子性气恶 - Means 'your son is very bad-tempered.'
  • 我留汝必遭炒閙 - Means 'if I let you stay, you'll cause a commotion.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 金溪 - A place name, likely in Jiangxi Province.
  • 绍熙二年 - The second year of the Shaoxi era (1191 AD) during the Southern Song Dynasty.
  • 金陵 - The ancient name for Nanjing, a major city in southern China.
  • 皂绨袍 - A black silk robe, a common garment for commoners.
  • 春寒 - Refers to the cold weather in early spring, which could affect agricultural activities.
  • 桑 - Mulberry trees, important for silk production and often associated with family life.

Proper Nouns

  • 许成 - A personal name, likely a commoner.
  • 孙鼎臣 - A personal name, likely a commoner.
  • 吴廿九 - A personal name, likely a commoner.

[405]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 不纳母出立檐下徬徨 - Describes a woman who is not allowed to enter and stands outside under the eaves, hesitating and wandering.
  • 至五更 - Refers to the fifth watch of the night, a time when it is very early morning.
  • 遥告虚天愿早天晓 - Means 'she called out to the empty sky, hoping for the dawn to come early.'
  • 俄闻鸡唱 - Means 'soon after, she heard the rooster crow.'
  • 发声泣 - Means 'she cried out in tears.'
  • 其妇言昨以桑叶之故被丈夫一夜打骂 - The wife says that her husband beat and scolded her all night because of the mulberry leaves.
  • 婆且去日正午片云起于山须臾烟雾四塞 - Describes the grandmother leaving, and at noon, a cloud rises from the mountain, soon turning into a dense fog.
  • 四人皆避于山下惊雷电掣有顷复晴 - Describes four people seeking shelter under the mountain due to a sudden thunderstorm, which clears up shortly after.
  • 三农聚首如初独吴不在走访之乃倒埋稻中入地二尺牢不可拔 - Describes three farmers gathering as usual, but Wu is missing; they find him buried upside down in the rice, two feet deep, firmly stuck.
  • 丘岑食蕈 - Refers to a scholar named Qiu Cen eating mushrooms.
  • 金溪士人丘岑就馆于乡家豪园仆居在傍使朝夕送馔仆尝得大蕈煑作羮一家五口暮而饱食之以一杯献岑岑曰此珍味也不可徒食命学生归求酒一升许满饮然后啜蕈 - Describes a scholar named Qiu Cen staying in a wealthy family's garden, where a servant prepares a large mushroom stew, and they all eat it at night. Qiu Cen, impressed, asks for wine to pair with the mushroom stew.
  • 既而闻仆夫妻皆呕血困仆往视之则两子复然不移时四人尽死 - After eating, the servant and his family all vomit blood and die.
  • 主人料岑必及祸畏其或殁于门内且为累亟置酒三觞延之偿其束修之直遣轿舁之归又饷以一尊主家去岑舍三十里行半涂与友生相遇因语其事遂留连共饮空其尊醉卧邸榻迨醒畧无所苦识者谓酒能解蕈毒云 - The host fears that Qiu Cen will also suffer and tries to get him drunk to protect him, but Qiu Cen remains unharmed, and it is believed that the wine neutralized the mushroom's poison.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 五更 - Refers to the traditional Chinese timekeeping system, dividing the night into five watches.
  • 鸡唱 - Refers to the rooster crowing, a common signal of dawn in traditional Chinese culture.
  • 桑叶之故 - Refers to an argument or conflict involving mulberry leaves, which might be related to silk production or agriculture.
  • 三农 - Refers to three farmers, a common term in classical Chinese literature to describe rural life.
  • 蕈 - Refers to mushrooms, which were sometimes used in traditional Chinese medicine and cuisine.
  • 束修之直 - Refers to the payment or compensation for a teacher or scholar.

Proper Nouns

  • 不纳母 - The name of the woman who is not allowed to enter.
  • 丘岑 - The name of the scholar.
  • 金溪 - The place where Qiu Cen is staying.
  • 乡家豪园 - The wealthy family's garden where Qiu Cen is staying.
  • 仆 - The servant who prepares the mushroom stew.
  • 吴 - One of the farmers who disappears.
  • 妻 - The wife of the servant.
  • 僧 - The monk who recites scriptures and offers prayers.

[406]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 缪夫人 - Title for a woman of noble status, likely a wife or concubine of a high-ranking official. The character '缪' (miù) suggests a respectful or formal address.
  • 罗春伯 - A proper name, likely referring to a high-ranking official. The character '春伯' (chūn bó) suggests a respectful or formal title.
  • 刑部尚书 - A high-ranking official in the imperial bureaucracy, specifically the head of the Ministry of Justice.
  • 绍熙五年 - The year 1194 in the reign of Emperor Xiaozong of Song.
  • 衰服 - Mourning attire, indicating a state of grief or loss.
  • 枢密告身 - A document granting a high-ranking official a position in the Chancellery of Military Affairs.
  • 入枢密府 - To enter the Chancellery of Military Affairs, indicating a promotion or change in official duties.
  • 婺州 - A prefecture in Zhejiang Province, modern-day Jinhua.
  • 太学生 - A student at the Imperial Academy, a prestigious institution for training future officials.
  • 大仙 - A term often used to refer to a spirit or deity, but in this context, it seems to refer to a fortune-teller or soothsayer.
  • 秋荐 - A reference to the autumn examination, a key part of the imperial examination system.
  • 木栢木 - Likely a type of wood, possibly used in the construction of coffins or other ceremonial objects.
  • 椿板 - A type of wooden board, possibly used in the construction of coffins or other ceremonial objects.
  • 防州 - A place name, likely referring to a specific region or administrative division.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 绍熙五年 - The year 1194, during the reign of Emperor Xiaozong of Song. This period was marked by significant political and cultural developments in the Song Dynasty.
  • 刑部尚书 - A high-ranking official in the imperial bureaucracy, responsible for overseeing the legal and judicial system.
  • 枢密告身 - A document granting a high-ranking official a position in the Chancellery of Military Affairs, which was a powerful institution in the Song Dynasty.
  • 婺州 - A prefecture in Zhejiang Province, known for its cultural and economic significance.
  • 太学生 - Students at the Imperial Academy, which was a prestigious institution for training future officials.
  • 大仙 - Often used to refer to a spirit or deity, but in this context, it seems to refer to a fortune-teller or soothsayer.

Proper Nouns

  • 缪夫人 - The title of the noblewoman.
  • 罗春伯 - The name of the high-ranking official.
  • 绍熙五年 - The year 1194.
  • 刑部尚书 - The title of the high-ranking official.
  • 婺州 - The prefecture in Zhejiang Province.
  • 太学生 - The title of the student at the Imperial Academy.
  • 大仙 - The term for the fortune-teller or soothsayer.
  • 防州 - The place name mentioned in the soothsayer's prediction.

[407]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 朱四客 - A proper name, likely referring to a person from the region of Wuxing (D婺).
  • 侍妾 - Refers to a concubine or secondary wife.
  • 毎岁必往视 - Means 'every year must go to visit.'
  • 一仆自随 - Means 'one servant follows him.'
  • 躯干甚伟 - Means 'the body is very strong and tall.'
  • 持长鎗 - Means 'holding a long spear.'
  • 叱朱四住 - Means 'shouting at Zhu Si to stop.'
  • 发其箧 - Means 'opening his box.'
  • 健勇有智 - Means 'strong, brave, and intelligent.'
  • 乗间自后引足蹴之 - Means 'taking advantage of an opportunity from behind, kicking him down.'
  • 就枕 - Means 'going to bed.'
  • 长叹曰 - Means 'sighed and said.'
  • 莫要説他 - Means 'don't talk about him.'
  • 具饭饷厥夫 - Means 'prepared food to feed his husband.'
  • 拊自心焉 - Means 'patting his own heart.'
  • 割壁而窜 - Means 'cutting through the wall and fleeing.'
  • 屏伏草问 - Means 'hiding in the grass and searching.'
  • 秉火求索 - Means 'holding a torch to search.'
  • 未几盗归 - Means 'not long after, the thief returned.'
  • 仓皇运水救火 - Means 'panicked and carried water to extinguish the fire.'
  • 复访朱 - Means 'to look for Zhu again.'
  • 遂尔得脱 - Means 'thus escaping.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 朱四客 - A proper name, possibly a historical figure or a fictional character.
  • 吴居甫 - A proper name, likely a historical figure or a fictional character.
  • 襄阳 - A city in Hubei Province, China.
  • 九江境 - Refers to the area around the Yangtze River.
  • 盗 - Refers to a thief or bandit.
  • 长鎗 - A long spear, a common weapon in ancient China.
  • 旅邸 - Refers to an inn or lodging place.
  • 媪 - Refers to an elderly woman.
  • 跛曳 - Means 'limping and dragging.'
  • 仓皇 - Means 'in a state of panic or haste.'

Proper Nouns

  • 朱四客 - A proper name, likely referring to a person from the region of Wuxing (D婺).
  • 吴居甫 - A proper name, likely a historical figure or a fictional character.
  • 襄阳 - A city in Hubei Province, China.
  • 九江境 - Refers to the area around the Yangtze River.
  • 媪 - Refers to an elderly woman.

[408]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 承宣使 - A high-ranking official title in the Song Dynasty, roughly equivalent to a provincial governor or military commander.
  • 挈家 - Means 'to take one's family with them,' indicating a journey involving dependents.
  • 干官 - Refers to an official position, likely a junior or advisory role.
  • 夜宿驿舍 - Means 'to stay overnight at an inn,' where *驿舍* refers to an official resting place for travelers.
  • 觉有贼 - Means 'to feel there are thieves,' where *觉* implies a sense of danger or suspicion.
  • 从吏呵骂之 - Means 'the servants shouted at them,' where *呵骂* is a strong rebuke.
  • 稍定 - Means 'gradually calmed down,' indicating a reduction in tension.
  • 试启户出视 - Means 'they cautiously opened the door to look outside,' where *试* implies caution.
  • 盘拏 - Means 'to wrestle or fight,' often used to describe animals fighting.
  • 舍舟趋陆 - Means 'to leave the boat and head for land,' indicating a change in mode of transportation.
  • 踞炉而坐 - Means 'to sit on the stove,' where *踞* implies a squatting or sitting posture.
  • 危愳防胆 - Means 'to be in great fear,' where *危愳防胆* emphasizes the intense fear.
  • 添薪益火 - Means 'to add more wood and increase the fire,' indicating efforts to maintain warmth.
  • 偃然自若 - Means 'to remain calm and composed,' where *偃然* suggests a relaxed state.
  • 奋迅而出 - Means 'to leap out with vigor,' where *奋迅* implies sudden and energetic movement.
  • 适来请火 - Means 'just came to borrow a light,' where *适来* indicates a recent arrival.
  • 骇仆而死 - Means 'scared and fell dead,' where *骇仆* implies a sudden and violent reaction.
  • 终夜危惧不安席 - Means 'spent the whole night in fear and unable to sleep,' where *不安席* indicates restlessness.
  • 辰巳间 - Refers to the hours of 7 AM to 9 AM, indicating the time of day.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 承宣使 - A high-ranking official in the Song Dynasty, responsible for military and administrative duties.
  • 绍熙四年 - Refers to the year 1193 in the reign of Emperor Guangzong of Song.
  • 临安 - The capital city of the Southern Song Dynasty, now known as Hangzhou.
  • 襄阳 - A strategic city in Hubei Province, often a site of military importance.
  • 都统司 - The headquarters of a military command, responsible for overseeing military operations.
  • 武昌 - A city in Hubei Province, historically significant for its role in military and political affairs.
  • 客舍 - A temporary lodging place for travelers, often less formal than an official *驿舍*.
  • 虎 - Tigers are often used in Chinese literature to symbolize danger and unpredictability.

Proper Nouns

  • 承宣使 - A high-ranking official title.
  • 孙珰 - The name of the official.
  • 禹功 - The name of the official's son.
  • 绍熙四年 - The year 1193.
  • 临安 - The capital city of the Southern Song Dynasty.
  • 襄阳 - A strategic city in Hubei Province.
  • 武昌 - A city in Hubei Province.

[409]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 善属文 - Literally means 'good at writing,' indicating a talent for literary composition.
  • 负俊声 - Means 'to have a reputation for excellence' or 'to be renowned for one's talent.'
  • 梦人称为 - Means 'to dream of someone calling oneself' or 'to have a dream where someone addresses oneself by a specific title.'
  • 自恃不浅 - Means 'to have a high opinion of oneself' or 'to be confident in one's abilities.'

  • 辄不偶 - Means 'always fails' or 'never succeeds,' indicating repeated failure in exams.

  • 怏怏而归 - Means 'to return home with a gloomy expression' or 'to go back with a sense of disappointment.'

  • 殊可观 - Means 'very impressive' or 'remarkably good.'

  • 耿介 - Means 'upright' or 'integrity,' describing a person of moral character.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 喜以死于非命 - Indicates that someone died in an unnatural or untimely manner.
  • 固非主母杀我 - A statement affirming that the death was not caused by the mistress.
  • 却自渠而发 - Indicates that the cause of death originated from another source.
  • 向者其福未衰 - Refers to the fact that the person's good fortune had not yet diminished.
  • 等守多年耳 - Suggests that the person had been waiting for a long time.
  • 潘许以斋醮经卷皆不应而作猫呌数声 - Describes a situation where Pan refused to perform rituals and instead made a cat cry out several times.
  • 童即昬昡及觉不能畧省 - Indicates that the child became dizzy and upon waking could not fully understand what happened.
  • 潘移牒城隍令放置鄷都宫 - Refers to Pan sending a message to the city god to place the deceased in the underworld.
  • 且咒治水与病者服似觉小愈 - Describes the use of spells to treat water-related diseases and illness, with some improvement.
  • 才数日复沈笃竟不起 - Indicates that after a few days, the condition worsened and the person did not recover.
  • 钱往吊防是夕梦妇人来曰我自报寃何与尔事 - Describes Qian going to mourn and having a dream where a woman comes and says she is seeking justice, which is unrelated to Qian.
  • 顾令潘法师囚我于狱非屈来地下作证不可旋抱疾少日亦亡 - Indicates that the woman orders Pan to imprison her in a dungeon, suggesting that she cannot be brought to the underworld for testimony unless forced, and then she falls ill and dies shortly after.
  • 噫防途业报茫茫理难致诘 - A lament about the unclear nature of karma and the difficulty in questioning it.
  • 庆喜之自死縁猫故乃贻祸主母及钱生则为太滥矣 - Suggests that the death of the cat led to misfortune for the mistress and Qian, implying that the consequences were excessive.
  • 岂命数相值偶然若是乎 - Questions whether the events were due to fate or mere coincidence.
  • 潘见卜 - Refers to Pan's practice of divination involving spirits.
  • 潘见者又启卜肆其事尤髙淳熙戊申岁禹之侄以前武陵通判诣阙当再得倅贰 - Describes Pan, who practices divination with spirits, opening a divination shop in the year of Wuxu during the Shaoxing era, and his nephew, who was previously a judge in Wuling, should be appointed as a deputy official.
  • 或云华亭胡朝散待建康次而得疾消息殊不佳 - Indicates that someone in Huating, a scholar named Hu, was waiting in Jiankang and fell ill, with the news being very unfavorable.
  • 若求代之只二年半便可仕 - Suggests that if someone seeks a replacement, they can be appointed in two and a half years.
  • 禹未能信将谋诣卜筮遂往访潘随其所需买香纸仍留白纸一幅于神堂祷请供事约明日复来 - Describes Yu, who was unsure and planned to consult a fortune-teller, visiting Pan and buying incense and paper, leaving a blank sheet of paper in the shrine for a prayer request, and agreeing to return the next day.
  • 既至潘取香炉灰反覆按纸上良久拂拭视之则成一方字 - Describes Yu returning and Pan taking the ashes from the incense burner, examining the paper for a long time, wiping it clean, and seeing a square character form.
  • 禹盖以胡君为问谓事不谐矣 - Indicates that Yu asked about Hu and concluded that the matter would not go well.
  • 时周益公在相 - Refers to the time when Zhou Yigong was in power.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 位往谒之伸建康之请 - Elliptical phrase; '位往' means 'the person went,' '谒之' means 'to visit him/her,' and '伸建康之请' means 'to make a request for Jiankang.'
  • 彼两倅阙皆是三四年 - '倅' refers to deputy officials, and '阙' means 'vacancy.' The phrase means 'both deputy positions have been vacant for three to four years.'
  • 何不求贑州 - '何不' means 'why not,' and '求' means 'seek.' The phrase means 'why not seek Ganzhou?'
  • 禹曰禹非不询究縁有饶金酉黄岩乡方盛三政官期甚逺 - '询究' means 'inquire thoroughly,' and '縁' means 'because.' The phrase means 'Yù said, 'I am not uninformed, but because there are many matters to attend to in Ráo, Jīn, Yǒu, Huáng, Yán, and Fāng, and the official period is far away.'
  • 周曰饶已不仕黄当即赴而为私计所牵见拟丐祠今但替方盛耳求者甚众出此日便落他人手后悔无及 - '不仕' means 'not serving,' '当即' means 'immediately,' '私计所牵' means 'tied up by personal considerations,' '丐祠' means 'request a temple post,' '替方盛耳' means 'to replace Fāng Shèng,' and '后悔无及' means 'it will be too late to regret.' The phrase means 'Zhōu said, 'Ráo has already stopped serving, Huang should immediately take up the position, but he is tied up by personal considerations and is planning to request a temple post. Now, it is only to replace Fāng Shèng. Many people are seeking this position, and if you don't take it today, it will fall into someone else's hands, and it will be too late to regret.'
  • 禹喜谢即纳劄子请之而心犹疑虑次日饶致仕命下黄遂祠请其到堂资歴同者争求之殆三十辈而禹劄在前遂得之于是证方字之验 - '喜谢' means 'gladly thanked,' '纳劄子' means 'submitted a petition,' '疑虑' means 'doubts,' '致仕' means 'retirement,' '遂祠请' means 'then requested a temple post,' '资歴同者' means 'those with the same qualifications,' '争求之' means 'competed to seek it,' '殆三十辈' means 'about thirty people,' and '证方字之验' means 'to verify the correctness of the character 'Fāng.' The phrase means 'Yù gladly thanked him and immediately submitted a petition to request it, but still had doubts. The next day, Ráo's retirement order was issued, and Huang then requested a temple post. When he arrived at the hall, those with the same qualifications competed to seek it, about thirty people in total, but Yù's petition was submitted first, so he got it. Thus, the correctness of the character 'Fāng' was verified.'
  • 夏书纸上曰事在千里外继书一食字一尧字合而读之则饶字也 - '书纸上' means 'wrote on the paper,' '事在千里外' means 'the matter is far away,' '食字' means 'the character '食,' '尧字' means 'the character '尧,' and '饶字' means 'the character '饶.' The phrase means 'Xia wrote on the paper, 'The matter is far away. Combine the characters '食' and '尧' to read it as '饶.''
  • 问曰是乎答曰然曰文书见系一多口人收得而鸳鸯为看守无足忧也 - '问曰是乎' means 'asked, 'Is it correct?'' '答曰然' means 'answered, 'Yes.' '文书见系一多口人收得' means 'the document is being held by a person with many mouths,' '鸳鸯为看守' means 'the mandarin ducks are guarding it,' and '无足忧也' means 'there is no need to worry.' The phrase means 'Asked, 'Is it correct?' Answered, 'Yes.' Said, 'The document is being held by a person with many mouths, and the mandarin ducks are guarding it. There is no need to worry.'
  • 其説茫洋无准的固以为妄既而仆从饶州来持所遗至盖向者打并行李时忘在外小妾福安见之介房中十箧用泥融飞燕子沙暖睡鸳鸯为标贴遂寘鸳鸯箧内既应鸳鸯看守之语而福字有口田字又四口所谓多口人者如是亦神矣哉 - '茫洋无准的' means 'vague and without definite meaning,' '固以为妄' means 'so it was considered false,' '仆从饶州来' means 'the servant came from Ráo,' '打并行李时忘在外' means 'forgot it when packing the luggage,' '小妾福安见之' means 'the concubine Fú'ān saw it,' '介房中十箧' means 'ten boxes in Jie's room,' '用泥融飞燕子沙暖睡鸳鸯为标贴' means 'used a clay mold of flying swallows and warm-sleeping mandarin ducks as a label,' '遂寘鸳鸯箧内' means 'so placed it in the mandarin ducks' box,' '应鸳鸯看守之语' means 'responding to the mandarin ducks' guarding words,' '福字有口田字又四口' means 'the character '福' has a mouth and a field, and another four mouths,' and '多口人者如是' means 'the person with many mouths is like this.' The phrase means 'The explanation was vague and without definite meaning, so it was considered false. Later, the servant came from Ráo with the forgotten document. It was forgotten when packing the luggage, and the concubine Fú'ān saw it. In Jie's room, there were ten boxes, and a clay mold of flying swallows and warm-sleeping mandarin ducks was used as a label. So, it was placed in the mandarin ducks' box, responding to the mandarin ducks' guarding words. The character '福' has a mouth and a field, and another four mouths, so the person with many mouths is like this. It is also miraculous!'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 建康 - The capital city of the Eastern Jin and Southern Dynasties, now known as Nanjing.
  • 贑州 - A prefecture in Jiangxi province.
  • 饶金酉黄岩乡方盛三政官期甚逺 - Refers to specific places and officials, indicating the complexity of administrative matters.
  • 夏巨源 - A person skilled in divination, likely a historical figure.
  • 多口人 - A metaphorical term, possibly referring to a person with many mouths, symbolizing gossip or a busy person.
  • 鸳鸯 - Mandarin ducks, often used as symbols of fidelity and protection.
  • 福安 - The name of a concubine, indicating a personal connection to the story.
  • 泥融飞燕子沙暖睡鸳鸯 - A poetic description, likely used as a label or symbol.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 李朝散 - Title referring to a scholar-official, 'chaosan' meaning 'scattered in the court,' often used for officials of a certain rank.
  • 监通判支盐仓 - Administrative duty, 'jian' means 'supervise,' 'tongpan' is an official title, and 'zhi yan cang' means 'salt warehouse.' Together, it refers to the supervision of salt warehouses.
  • 海多产蛏 - Means 'the sea produces many clams,' 'cheng' is a type of clam.
  • 酢醢 - Refers to a type of vinegar and meat sauce, often used as a condiment.
  • 饷遗亲故 - Means 'to send gifts to relatives and old friends.'
  • 颇多 - Means 'quite a lot' or 'a great deal.'
  • 梦若游他处 - Means 'to dream as if wandering elsewhere.'
  • 官府见神人冕服正坐 - Means 'in the official office, a divine person with a ceremonial robe and hat sits upright.'
  • 汝近日何得广杀生 - Means 'why have you recently killed so many living beings?'
  • 合减一筭 - Means 'you should be reduced by one calculation' or 'you should be penalized by one calculation.'
  • 吾念汝善也从恕 - Means 'I think you are good, so I will forgive you.'
  • 自后当力戒之 - Means 'from now on, you should strongly refrain from it.'
  • 吾姓俞氏 - Means 'my surname is Yu.'
  • 仕至宣教郎 - Means 'I served up to the rank of Xuanjiao Lang.'
  • 年劳当升朝已下磨勘 - Means 'due to years of service, I should have been promoted to the rank of Chaoyi (a lower official) and undergo evaluation.'
  • 通直命下曽不克拜而终 - Means 'the order for Tongzhi (a higher official) was issued, but I was unable to bow and received it.'
  • 掌百禽兽水族 - Means 'to manage all birds, beasts, and aquatic creatures.'
  • 行且受代 - Means 'I am about to be replaced.'
  • 生前与汝为姻戚 - Means 'before death, I was related to you by marriage.'
  • 特告汝 - Means 'I specially tell you this.'
  • 遂辞去 - Means 'then he left.'
  • 间有语曰 - Means 'there was a brief statement that said.'
  • 看经与道士着 - Means 'reading scriptures and wearing the attire of a Taoist priest.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 而思之俞乃其妻族名从立人者妻之诸祖也 - Complex and elliptical phrase meaning 'thinking about it, Yu was the name of the wife’s clan, which followed the ‘立人’ (standing person) character, and she was the wife of the ancestors of the Yu clan.'
  • 但不晓去后之説 - Means 'but I did not understand the explanation after his departure.'
  • 明日问土人始知蛏有两种 - Means 'the next day, I asked the locals and learned that there are two types of clams.'
  • 小曰孩儿蛏大曰道士蛏 - Means 'the small ones are called ‘child clams,’ and the large ones are called ‘Taoist clams.’'
  • 昨日所制盖择取其大者 - Means 'yesterday’s preparation was selecting the larger ones.'
  • 亟唤僧诵经资荐之 - Means 'I immediately called a monk to recite sutras to offer merit for him.'
  • 迨秩满还家言于诸俞云 - Means 'when my term was completed and I returned home, I told the Yu clan about it.'
  • 临欲迁官而捐馆未几告其妻曰 - Means 'just before he was to be promoted, he died. Soon after, he told his wife.'
  • 我今为神实掌鳞羽诸兽 - Means 'I am now a spirit and truly oversee all fish, birds, and beasts.'
  • 而为我造冕服 - Means 'and have them make a ceremonial cap and robe for me.'
  • 妻如其请仿祠庙中规范绘饰设斋祭供焚而献之 - Means 'his wife followed his request, imitating the norms of the temple, decorating, setting up a斋祭 (ritual sacrifice), and burning offerings to present them.'
  • 至辛巳岁垂四十年矣而灵着如此 - Means 'by the year Xin Si (1181 CE), it had been nearly forty years, and his spirit was so powerful.'
  • 俞之侄孙瀹刻石记其事 - Means 'Yu’s great-nephew E carved a stone tablet to record the event.'
  • 淳熙十四年春江淮浙疠气肆行但不甚为害 - Means 'In the spring of the fourteenth year of the Chongxi era (1187 CE), the epidemic spread throughout Jianghuai and Zhejiang, but it was not very harmful.'
  • 惟中者觉头痛身不过三日即愈 - Means 'only those who were affected felt headaches and body aches, but they recovered within three days.'
  • 名为虼蟇瘟言自淮北来 - Means 'it was called the ‘gecko plague,’ said to have come from the north of the Huai River.'
  • 赵师缙明叔云其祖彦泽镇州正坐决事一吏以疾作告俄纷纷继之过半不止 - Means 'Zhao Shijin, Ming Shu, said that his ancestor Yanze, who was stationed in Yangzhou, was deciding a matter when a clerk fell ill, and soon after, many more fell ill, more than half of them.'
  • 明日趋庭之吏三分仅有其一 - Means 'the next day, of the officials who went to court, only one in three remained.'
  • 当昼宴客一倡方行酒亦以此去 - Means 'during the day, while entertaining guests, one of the entertainers was about to serve wine, but they also fell ill and left.'
  • 迨终席无一人在 - Means 'by the end of the banquet, no one was left.'
  • 翁潾云时为溧水主簿身遭其沴 - Means 'Weng Lin, who was at the time the magistrate of Lizhui, suffered from the plague himself.'
  • 既而举邑尽然 - Means 'soon after, the entire county was affected.'
  • 予在翰林大儿自乡里携妇来省至衢买舟而方离岸即有病者浸浸舟中之人无有得免者 - Means 'I was in the Hanlin Academy, and my eldest son came from the countryside to visit me. When we bought a boat in Qu, just as we were about to leave the shore, some of the people on the boat fell ill, and none were spared.'
  • 然不药而愈 - Means 'but they recovered without medicine.'
  • 所在相传云顷年未尝如是也 - Means 'it was said that this had never happened before.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 俞 - A clan name, likely referring to a prominent family in the region.
  • 蛏 - A type of clam, with specific names for different sizes.
  • 宣教郎 - An official title in the Song Dynasty, typically a low-ranking civil servant.
  • 淳熙 - The era name of Emperor Xiaozong of Song (1163–1189 CE).
  • 疠气 - Refers to an epidemic or plague.
  • 虼蟇瘟 - A specific type of plague, possibly named after a local superstition or belief.
  • 赵师缙明叔 - Refers to Zhao Shijin, a historical figure, and his ancestor Yanze, who served as a local official.
  • 溧水主簿 - A local official position, equivalent to a county clerk or magistrate.
  • 翰林 - Refers to the Hanlin Academy, an elite scholarly institution in the imperial court.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 忧其复尔不暇出声唤仆 - Zhao was worried it would do it again and did not have time to call out for help.
  • 会帅宴已散知其故责厥子曰奈何将人命为戏 - The commander's banquet had ended, and he learned the reason, scolding Zhao's son, saying, 'How could you treat human life as a game?'

  • 卒窘甚驻立语之 - The soldier was extremely anxious and stood still, speaking to the tiger.
  • 卒到他铺交还递毕因留宿与彼中人言自喜再生 - The soldier arrived at another relay station, handed over the document, and stayed overnight, telling the people there that he was glad to be reborn.
  • 明日囬至昨处虎复相遇竟为所食 - The next day, when he returned to the previous place, he met the tiger again and was ultimately eaten.
  • 方知命分当死于虎畴昔之免端为文书故云 - He realized that his fate was to die at the hands of the tiger, and his earlier escape was due to the imperial decree.

  • 方知命分当死于虎畴昔之免端为文书故云 - The realization of the soldier's fate and the explanation for his earlier escape provide a moral lesson.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 寝程初不从 - Unclear due to the character ''; could mean '程最初不愿意' (Cheng initially did not agree).
  • 我便走投都保説汝掠我来强奸我 - Direct statement of violence and coercion; '我便走投都保' could mean 'I would then go to the city guard' or 'I would seek help from the city guard.'
  • 程颇惧又覩其容色胜厥妻乃遂其请 - '程颇惧' means 'Cheng was very afraid,' '覩其容色胜厥妻' means 'saw her appearance surpass his wife,' and '乃遂其请' means 'and thus agreed to her request.'
  • 将晓复云我后须到汝家若怕妻不容自向近处别讨一住屋但时时过来相就亦得 - '将晓' means 'at dawn,' '复云' means 'again said,' '我后须到汝家' means 'I will need to come to your house,' '若怕妻不容' means 'if you fear your wife won't allow it,' '自向近处别讨一住屋' means 'find another place to live nearby,' '但时时过来相就亦得' means 'but you can still come over often.'
  • 临别于手帕内取一衲袄与程 - '临别' means 'at the time of parting,' '于手帕内取一衲袄' means 'took out a patched coat from the handkerchief,' '与程' means 'and gave it to Cheng.'
  • 程既还家不以语母妻 - '程既还家' means 'Cheng returned home,' '不以语母妻' means 'did not tell his mother and wife.'
  • 所居五里外有虚市曰广平距邑十五里 - '所居五里外' means 'five miles away from where he lived,' '有虚市' means 'there was a market,' '曰广平' means 'called Guangping,' '距邑十五里' means 'fifteen miles from the city.'
  • 程日一往忽被疾始与母道所见曰彼妇恰入房相存问坐床上移时方出门去 - '程日一往' means 'Cheng went there every day,' '忽被疾' means 'suddenly fell ill,' '始与母道所见' means 'then told his mother what he saw,' '彼妇恰入房' means 'that woman just entered the room,' '相存问' means 'to inquire about each other,' '坐床上移时' means 'sat on the bed for a while,' '方出门去' means 'finally left the house.'
  • 迨晓而死母殓以衲袄而塟之 - '迨晓' means 'until dawn,' '而死' means 'and died,' '母殓以衲袄' means 'his mother dressed him in the patched coat,' '而塟之' means 'and buried him.'
  • 妻后更适人惟生一女及嫁程母往依遂空一室 - '妻后更适人' means 'his wife later remarried,' '惟生一女' means 'only had one daughter,' '及嫁' means 'when she married,' '程母往依' means 'Cheng's mother went to live with her,' '遂空一室' means 'and left the house empty.'

  • 此外即无强占聴得里声押王大出对 - '聴得' means 'heard,' and '里声' means 'rumors in the village.' '押王大出对' means 'forcing 王大 to appear in court.'

  • 经一岁又如缙云周氏 - After a year, he went back to the Zhou family in Jinyun. '经一岁' means 'after a year,' and '如缙云周氏' means 'went back to the Zhou family in Jinyun.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 绍熙四年至衢州诣市驵赵十三家所赍直三百千 - This sentence describes the specific year and the amount of money Wang carried.

  • 常日奉事僧伽大圣虽出行亦以画象自随旦暮香火瞻事 - This sentence describes Wang's religious practices.
  • 俟半夜人定欲投诸深渊将出户有僧数人继踵来惧其见也为之少止良久再出则遇僧如初凡五六返且天明不暇顾径舁至江滨 - This sentence describes Zhao's plan and the interference of monks.

  • 何得厮欺负两人喧争不已 - Colloquial expression meaning 'How can they bully each other, with the two of them quarreling endlessly?'
  • 道士批彦颊 - Literally 'the Taoist slapped Yan's cheek,' where '批' means to slap or strike.
  • 彦不知端由未敢报 - 'Yan did not know the reason and dared not retaliate,' where '端由' means the cause or reason.
  • 但以手搦其腕 - 'But he grasped his wrist with his hand,' where '搦' means to grasp or hold tightly.
  • 道士不能敌顾而言曰 - 'The Taoist could not withstand it and then said,' where '顾而言曰' means to turn around and say.
  • 且舍汝去今夜三更后当使汝知我神通耳 - 'Let me leave you for now, but tonight after the third watch, I will make you know my divine power,' where '三更' refers to the third watch of the night, roughly midnight to 2 AM.
  • 彦归舍情思弗安 - 'Yan returned home and felt uneasy in mind,' where '情思弗安' means feeling anxious or troubled.
  • 于是迎师巫考治皆不效 - 'So they invited a shaman to examine and treat him, but it was ineffective,' where '考治' means to examine and treat.
  • 奄奄百许日得五雷陈法师怪乃谢去 - 'After a hundred or so days, he finally recovered, and the Five Thunder Chen Taoist, finding it strange, then left,' where '奄奄' means slowly recovering, and '谢去' means to leave in gratitude or due to a change in circumstances.
  • 李梦旦兄弟 - 'Li Mengdan's brothers,' where '兄弟' can refer to siblings or close companions.
  • 饶州学生李梦旦家庆元元年五月日尽病疫 - 'The student Li Mengdan from Raozhou, in the first month of the first year of the Jiaqing era, was afflicted with an epidemic,' where '庆元' is the era name of Emperor Gaozong of Song.
  • 唯其弟梦说得免 - 'Only his younger brother Mengshuo escaped,' where '唯' means only or merely.
  • 一门内外米盐百役弟悉任之 - 'All the household affairs, both inside and out, including buying rice and salt, were taken care of by the younger brother,' where '米盐' refers to basic necessities like food and salt.
  • 先是旦卧病虽剧然五日即愈 - 'Before this, when Mengdan was sick, although severely ill, he recovered in five days,' where '剧' means severe or intense.
  • 梦中见神人相与言 - 'In his dream, he saw a divine person speaking,' where '相与言' means to converse or speak together.
  • 梦说亦合有五日病 - 'Mengshuo should also have a five-day illness,' where '合' means should or ought to.
  • 但他若不安此家事务无人掌管如何 - 'But if he is not well, who will manage the household affairs?' where '掌管' means to manage or oversee.
  • 傍一人云不教渠兄替 - 'Someone nearby said, 'Do not let his elder brother take over,' where '渠' means him or it, and '替' means to replace or take over.
  • 神曰可便觉遍体火其病如初 - 'The divine person said, 'Then you will immediately feel all over burning, and your illness will return to its original state,' where '' is an archaic character for 'hot' or 'burning.'
  • 经四日梦中具状告神只乞免余日 - 'After four days, in his dream, he presented a petition to the divine person, only asking to be exempt from the remaining days,' where '具状' means to present a petition or complaint.

  • 绍兴中张魏公镇闽母兾夫人多以度牒付东禅寺使择其徒披剃长老梦黒龙蟠踞寺外旦而觇之张也问之曰欲为僧乎曰固所愿于是落发名圆觉 - Zhang's ordination and the dream that led to it: 'During the Shaoxing period, Zhang Wei Gong governed Fujian. His mother, Lady Qi, often gave ordination certificates to the East Zen Temple, asking them to choose a disciple to be ordained. One night, the abbot dreamed of a black dragon coiled outside the temple. The next morning, he saw Zhang and asked, 'Do you want to become a monk?' Zhang replied, 'I would like to.' So, he shaved his head and was given the name Yuanjue.'
  • 尝以双拳纳口中每笑时几至于剧素不识字而时时赋诗 - Zhang's unique habits and poetic talent: 'He often put both fists in his mouth, and when he laughed, it was almost uncontrollable. Despite not knowing how to read or write, he often composed poetry.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 孫提舉 - A title, likely *Tujiu* (提舉), a government office or official position in the Song Dynasty. The name '孫' is the surname, so 'Sun Tujiu' would be a proper rendering.
  • 家居臨安 - Lived in Lin'an, referring to the capital of the Southern Song Dynasty, which was renamed Lin'an (modern Hangzhou).
  • 既殁之后 - 'After his death,' indicating the time when the events take place.
  • 孤弱 - 'Orphaned and weak,' describing the state of the wife and children after the husband's death.
  • 美色 - 'Beautiful appearance,' used to describe the daughters.
  • 先亡 - 'Died earlier,' referring to the eldest daughter.
  • 同宗养女 - 'Adopted by a relative of the same clan,' indicating the fourth daughter was adopted into the family.
  • 流落于永阳县王后院 - 'Wandered to the Wáng Hòuyuàn (Wang's rear courtyard) in Yong County. The term *王后院* is unclear but likely refers to a place of residence or a servant's quarters, possibly a nunnery or a noble estate.'
  • 乾道元年 - The first year of the Qiandao era, referring to the reign of Emperor Xiaozong of Song (1165).
  • 浙西大疫 - 'A great plague in western Zhejiang,' indicating a large-scale epidemic.
  • 并妇及第二第三女死焉 - 'And his wife, as well as the second and third daughters, died.' The structure is elliptical, but the meaning is clear.
  • 妻虑祸未艾 - 'The wife feared the calamity was not yet over.' *未艾* means 'not yet ended.'
  • 以为长女墓不吉所致 - 'Believing that the calamity was caused by the inauspiciousness of the eldest daughter's tomb.'
  • 遣所亲少年魏二官人 - 'Sent her close relative, the young man Wei Er, who is called *guanren*.' *官人* is a term of respect or a title, often used for a husband, but here it likely refers to a respected or official-seeming person.
  • 往新市举焚其柩 - 'To go to Xinshi and raise the coffin to burn it.' *举焚* is a verb phrase meaning 'to lift and burn.'
  • 守庵老尼 - 'An old nun who lived in a temple or庵 (monastery).' *庵* is a small Buddhist nunnery.
  • 天行炽毒 - 'The epidemic is rampant and virulent.' *天行* refers to diseases caused by heaven or fate, *炽毒* means 'intense poison.'
  • 谁家不壊人口 - 'Who’s family is not affected by the loss of people?' *壊人口* is a euphemistic way of saying 'loss of people' or 'deaths.'
  • 大小娘子入土数载 - 'The *da xiao nangzi* (big and small ladies) have been buried for several years.' *大小娘子* is a term for the deceased daughters, likely used respectfully.
  • 幸自宁帖 - 'Happily at rest,' implying peace in the afterlife.
  • 岂忍无故残暴其朽骨 - 'How could we endure to brutally destroy their decayed bones?' *残暴* is a strong term, suggesting cruelty.
  • 以起泉下之寃愤哉 - 'To provoke the resentment of the dead?' *泉下* means 'in the netherworld' or 'underground,' and *寃愤* means 'grievance or resentment.'
  • 吾亦何心 - 'I have no intention,' or 'I have no heart to do this.'
  • 但奉宜人命为此 - 'But I am merely carrying out the command of the *yiren*.' *宜人* is a respectful title for a wife or a noblewoman.
  • 讵容空囬 - 'How could I return empty-handed?' *讵容* is a rhetorical question meaning 'how could one possibly.'
  • 闭拒再三不能遏 - 'She closed the door and resisted repeatedly, unable to be stopped.'
  • 待与你説明旦来可也 - 'Wait, I will explain. Come tomorrow.' *説明* is 'to explain,' and *旦* is 'morning.'
  • 姑应曰诺 - 'Temporarily replied 'yes' or 'I agree.'
  • 遂去 - 'Then left.'
  • 自之后 - 'After her burial,' with ** being the character for *sù* (burial or interment).
  • 夜出至尼房问讯酬答 - 'At night, she would go to the nun’s room to visit and exchange greetings.'
  • 聴其诵经 - 'Listen to her recite sutras.'
  • 迨三四更始退 - 'Until the third or fourth watch of the night, then she would leave.' *更* is a unit of time in the night, with five watches in total.
  • 是夜亦至尼 - 'That night, she also went to the nun.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 提舉 - A Song Dynasty official title, often translated as 'Superintendent' or 'Inspector.' It was a mid-level government position, and the holder would have had administrative or supervisory duties.
  • 臨安 - The capital of the Southern Song Dynasty, located in modern-day Hangzhou.
  • 乾道元年 - The first year of the Qiandao era, which was the reign period of Emperor Xiaozong of Song (r. 1162–1189).
  • 浙西 - Western Zhejiang, referring to the western region of the Zhejiang province in China.
  • 永阳县 - A county in the Song Dynasty, likely in the Jiangxi or Hunan region, though the exact location is unclear.
  • 王后院 - A term possibly referring to a residence, a servant's quarters, or a nunnery. The term *后院* can mean 'rear courtyard' or 'back part of the estate.'
  • 宜人 - A respectful title used for a wife or a noblewoman, often equivalent to 'lady' or 'madam.'
  • 尼 - A nun or a Buddhist nun. *庵 (an)* is a small nunnery or temple.
  • 诵经 - To recite sutras or Buddhist scriptures.
  • 更 - A unit of time used in the night, with five *geng* in one night, each lasting about two hours.
  • 墓不吉 - The belief that the location or construction of a tomb could bring misfortune or calamity, a common superstition in traditional Chinese culture.

  • 知人且至则粘着石上牢不可拔 - If they know someone is coming, they stick to the rocks and cannot be pulled off.
  • 虽椎撃至碎亦然 - Even if they are hit with a hammer until they break, they still stick.
  • 案后汉书伏隆张步献鳆鱼郭璞注三苍曰鳆似蛤偏着石广志曰鳆有殻一面附石细孔杂杂或七或九与此説同 - Citation from the *Book of the Later Han*, *Biography of Fu Long*, where Zhang Bu presented abalone. Guo Pu's annotation in *San Cang* says abalone is like a clam and sticks to the rock. *Guang Zhi* also says abalone has a shell and one side is attached to the rock with fine holes, sometimes seven or nine, which agrees with this statement.
  • 若江瑶淡菜之属取之甚易 - Like sea snails and clams, which are very easy to catch.
  • 迨欲出水则循绳扳縁足蹑以升 - When they want to come out of the water, they climb up by holding onto the rope and stepping with their feet.
  • 或久而不出而有泡沫堆突起于水面者 - Sometimes they don't come out for a long time, and bubbles form on the surface of the water.
  • 妻子在舟中见其状皆抚胷恸哭 - Their wives and children in the boat see this and all cry out in sorrow.
  • 盖已为大鱼衔去矣 - It seems they have been taken by a big fish.
  • 遭此者常常有之 - Those who encounter this often happen.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 为人害居民共邀俊施术 - Residents who are harmed by something invite Mr. Jun to perform a ritual. This is a colloquial expression indicating that the locals sought help from Mr. Jun due to a problem.
  • 在吾法不宜率尔盍具状以来 - According to my method, it is not appropriate to act rashly; instead, prepare a formal petition. This suggests a formal and cautious approach.
  • 书章奏天 - Submit a written petition to heaven. This is a ritualistic action to seek divine intervention.
  • 诘旦诣穴口为坛 - At dawn, go to the entrance of the cave and set up an altar. This indicates a specific time and location for the ritual.
  • 被发跣足衣道士服 - Wear a Taoist priest's robe, barefoot, and with hair loose. This describes the attire for the ritual.
  • 向空叱神将 - Shout at the empty space to summon the divine generals. This is a magical incantation to call upon divine assistance.
  • 速斯须蛇不出继遣两将如是者三四反 - Summon the snake quickly; if it does not appear, send two more generals, repeating this process several times. This describes a series of summoning attempts.
  • 蛇猛从穴内奋迅奔坛若将欲鬭者 - The snake rushes out from the cave with great speed, as if it is about to fight. This describes the snake's aggressive behavior.
  • 俊大声诃之曰业畜那得无礼 - Mr. Jun loudly rebukes it, saying, 'You wicked beast, how dare you be disrespectful?' This is a strong rebuke to the snake.
  • 取所着汗衫中分裂其裾 - Take his sweatshirt and tear it into pieces. This describes a ritual action to punish the snake.
  • 此患遂绝 - This trouble is thus resolved. This indicates the resolution of the problem.
  • 民家小儿因行草际遭螫毒彻心腑几于不救 - A child from a local family, while walking in the grass, is stung by a venomous snake, nearly dying. This describes a tragic incident.
  • 俊往疗之问儿曰汝误踏践之以致齧将自行其傍而然 - Mr. Jun goes to treat the child and asks, 'Did you accidentally step on it, causing it to bite you? Did you approach it closely?' This is a series of questions to understand the cause of the incident.
  • 初未尝触之不觉咬我 - I did not touch it; I did not feel it bite me. This is the child's response, indicating a lack of awareness.
  • 我亦久知之此无故伤人 - I have known this for a long time; it has no reason to harm people. This is Mr. Jun's statement, indicating his awareness of the snake's nature.
  • 命不可恕乃除地丈许插小竹片为劎作法呼蛇至者如积 - It cannot be forgiven; clear an area of about a meter and insert small bamboo strips as swords, then perform a ritual to summon the snakes. This describes a more severe ritual to address the problem.
  • 令之曰作过者留劎下否则退 - Command them, 'Those who have committed wrongs must stay under the swords; otherwise, they must leave.' This is a command to the snakes.
  • 群蛇以次相引而去各适所在 - The snakes leave one by one, each going to its respective place. This describes the snakes' departure.
  • 独一小蛇色如土伏劎傍 - Only a small snake, colored like earth, remains near the sword. This describes the last remaining snake.
  • 俊召判官检法曰蛇无故伤人当何罪 - Mr. Jun calls the judge to check the law, saying, 'What punishment should be given to a snake that harms people without cause?' This is a legal inquiry.
  • 俊之技如此而无所求于人 - Mr. Jun's skill is like this, and he does not seek anything from people. This summarizes Mr. Jun's character.
  • 医士刘大用欲学其术俊曰此非所靳但虑持之不谨或干犯法律将自贻祸乃止 - The doctor Liu Dayong wants to learn this skill; Mr. Jun says, 'This is not something to be stingy about, but I fear that if you handle it carelessly, you may violate the law and bring disaster upon yourself, so I will not teach you.' This is Mr. Jun's refusal to teach the skill.
  • 景陈弟云乡里亦曾有猪豚蛇以身腯而短不能蜿蜒故惟直前冲人遭之者无活理盖虺蝮也 - Mr. Jing Chen's younger brother says that in the village, there was once a snake that was fat and short, unable to move gracefully, so it would only rush forward and attack people, making it impossible to survive if one was bitten. This was likely a type of venomous snake. This provides additional context about the snake.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 池州白衣男子 - White-clad man from Chizhou; refers to an unidentified man from the city of Chizhou who appears in the story. The term '白衣' (white-clad) may carry connotations of anonymity or otherworldliness.
  • 诣其家饮酒托宿 - Visited her home, drank wine, and asked to stay overnight; describes the man's actions in a straightforward manner.
  • 相得甚欢 - Got along very well; indicates a positive relationship between the man and the woman.
  • 逾三月久 - For more than three months; specifies the duration of their relationship.
  • 母之防从之求物 - Because of my mother’s precautions, I ask for an object; reflects the woman’s reluctance to openly seek something from the man.
  • 明日持与汝 - I will bring it to you tomorrow; is the man’s promise.
  • 竟令尾其后 - She finally ordered her servant to follow him; indicates the woman’s persistence in tracking the man.
  • 迤逦出郭门西 - Gradually left the city gate to the west; describes the man’s departure.
  • 木下三郎庙前 - In front of the Temple of Sanlang; refers to a specific location where the man stopped.
  • 可囬头有亲家叫汝 - Turn around, there is a relative calling you; is the man’s instruction to the servant.
  • 但见大白蛇望茅冈疾趋 - Only saw a large white snake rushing towards the grassy hill; describes a supernatural event.
  • 骇颤欲仆 - Frightened and trembling, almost falling; captures the servant’s reaction.
  • 鬻于染肆为妾 - Sold to a dye shop as a concubine; indicates the woman’s subsequent fate.
  • 陈氏鬼疰 - Chen’s ghostly ailment; refers to the woman’s mysterious illness.
  • 韶州南七十里曰古田 - Seventy miles south of Shaoguan is called Guti; provides geographical context.
  • 抱异疾 - Suffering from a strange illness; describes the woman’s condition.
  • 每遇防风吹拂 - Whenever a cold wind blows; sets the context for the onset of her symptoms.
  • 举体皆然 - The whole body becomes like that; indicates the spreading nature of the illness.
  • 逮于发厥 - Until she loses consciousness; describes the severity of the illness.
  • 有声如欬 - A sound like coughing; describes the noise she makes when she regains consciousness.
  • 乍前乍后若摇兀之状 - Moving forward and backward as if swaying; describes her movements after regaining consciousness.
  • 率以百数 - Usually happens dozens of times; indicates the frequency of her episodes.
  • 甫小定又经日始困卧不知人 - Just as it slightly calms down, she falls into a deep sleep and loses consciousness again; describes the cyclical nature of her illness.
  • 累夕而愈 - After several nights, she recovers; indicates the duration of her recovery.
  • 至不敢出户 - She becomes so afraid that she dares not leave her house; shows the impact of the illness on her life.
  • 吾已得其证矣 - I have already diagnosed her condition; indicates the doctor’s confidence in his diagnosis.
  • 先与药一服 - First give her a dose of medicine; describes the initial treatment.
  • 命取数珠一串来 - Order to bring a string of prayer beads; is a unique instruction that requires further research.
  • 疏数之节 - The rhythm of the beads; refers to the timing of the beads’ movement, which the doctor uses to diagnose the illness.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 民疾者十室而九 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'nine out of ten households were affected by the disease,' emphasizing the widespread nature of the epidemic.
  • 张多治善药分诸坊曲散给而求者絶少 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'Zhang provided a lot of good medicine, distributed it among the neighborhoods, but very few people came to ask for it,' suggesting a lack of awareness or fear.
  • 凡有疾者必使来致祷戒令不得服药 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'all those who were ill were required to come and pray, and they were forbidden to take medicine,' indicating a reliance on religious practices rather than medical treatment.
  • 故虽府中给施而不敢请 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'therefore, even though the government provided medicine, people did not dare to ask for it,' highlighting the fear of the witches.
  • 他日至岳祠奠谒户庭悄悄香火寥落 - Idiomatic expression describing a visit to the Dongyue temple where the courtyard was quiet and the incense was sparse, indicating a lack of activity.
  • 问瘟庙所在从吏谓必加瞻仰命炷香设褥张悉彻去 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'he asked about the location of the plague temple, and the officials said they would surely pay homage, but Zhang ordered all the incense and cushions to be removed,' showing his determination to change the situation.
  • 时老弱妇女祈赛阗咽见使君来争丛绕环视 - Idiomatic expression describing the scene where elderly women and children gathered around Zhang, indicating their curiosity and possibly their hope for help.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 张子智贵谟知常州庆元乙夘 - Refers to Zhang Zizhi, a governor of Changzhou during the Song Dynasty, specifically in the year 1195 (乙夘).
  • 东岳行宫 - A temple dedicated to the God of the East Peak (Dongyue), a significant deity in Chinese folk religion.
  • 瘟神 - Refers to the plague god, a figure in Chinese mythology associated with disease and epidemics.
  • 四巫 - Refers to four witches or sorcerers who were believed to have control over the plague.
  • 府中给施 - Means 'the government provided medicine,' reflecting the role of the government in public health during the Song Dynasty.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The long sentence '宣和六年江遐举邈为隆徳府教授求学司檄搜遗书与家人及长子珪诣五台山宿于文殊院' describes the year, the person, the position, the task, and the destination, and must be read as a continuous narrative.
  • The clause '明日斋罢拜祷佛像前乞现灵相' describes the next day's activities, including prayer and the request for a miraculous appearance.
  • The long sequence '至晡时凉风防动西边淡黄云起少焉声闻罗汉天众各执幡幢香华以次引导戈甲旌旗陆续不絶傍与日光相映' describes the atmospheric conditions and the appearance of various supernatural beings, and must be read as a continuous description.
  • The clause '最后菩萨乘师子法座四夷君长氊裘狼帽谨随之无量无边各随其异香芬郁鼻观为清经数刻乃没' describes the final appearance of the Bodhisattva and the various beings that follow, and must be read as a continuous description.
  • The clause '天将敛昏又见灯烛数百炬陈列金紫贵人执手炉行前菩萨缓步足迹所留莲花随生一黄犬摇尾在后' describes the evening scene and the appearance of the Bodhisattva, and must be read as a continuous description.
  • The clause '披僧衣亦现像侧仙女吹箫笙可闻' describes the appearance of a monk and fairies, and must be read as a continuous description.
  • The long sequence '江一家并僧仆无不瞻覩靖康之冬报代马饮河弃官赴京师除太学博士居竹栅巷未几梦菩萨告以急请外即挈家东下出水门值泗上便舟防汴岸遂迤逦还严陵阖门安堵时京师已受敌矣间闗乱离不受怖恐' describes the family's experiences and the political situation, and must be read as a continuous narrative.
  • The clause '珪生于政和癸巳至庆元丙辰八十有四嵗犹强健不衰' describes the lifespan and condition of Gui, and must be read as a continuous narrative.

  • 棺中 - The coffin, which in Chinese culture is often seen as a place where personal items and important documents can be placed to accompany the deceased in the afterlife.

  • 黄师宪祷梨山 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'Huang Shixian Prays at Li Mountain.'
  • 赴省试 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'to take the provincial examination.'
  • 与里中陈应求约同行 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'to make an agreement with his fellow villager Chen Yingqiu to travel together.'
  • 以事未办集 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'because certain matters were not yet completed.'
  • 后数日乃登涂 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'after several days he finally set out on the road.'
  • 诣梨山李侯庙谒梦 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'to visit the Li Hou Temple on Li Mountain and seek a dream.'
  • 梦神告曰 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'the spirit in the dream told him.'
  • 不必吾有言 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'you do not need to hear what I say.'
  • 只见陈俊卿他所说者是已 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'just see what Chen Junqing said is correct.'
  • 方与陈防即询其得失 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'when he met Chen Fang, he immediately asked about the results.'
  • 辞以不能知 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'Chen Fang replied that he did not know.'
  • 道之不已 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'continued to urge him.'
  • 怒大声咄曰 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'angrily shouted loudly.'
  • 师宪做第一人俊卿居其次足矣 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'Shixian will be the first, Junqing will be second, that is enough.'
  • 喜其与梦合乃以告之 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'he was pleased that it matched his dream and told him.'
  • 暨掲牓如其说 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'when the results were announced, they matched what he had said.'
  • 太嵗堂 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'The Tai Sui Hall.'
  • 姑苏张比部家极富盛名园甲第冠于二浙 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'The family of Zhang Bibu in Suzhou was extremely wealthy and famous, their gardens and mansions were the best in the two Zhe regions.'
  • 崇宁间于后圃起华堂前凿大池取其土以筑堂阯 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'During the Chongning era, they built a magnificent hall in the back garden and dug a large pond in front of it, using the soil to build the foundation of the hall.'
  • 掘地数尺得一蛇细财如箸然蟠结穹隆其长不可胜计 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'After digging several feet into the ground, they found a snake as thin as a chopstick, coiled and forming a dome, its length was uncountable.'
  • 比部之子实主此役畧不以物恠介抱 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'The son of Bibu was in charge of this project and did not consider the strange object a problem.'
  • 命仆夫断为数百截而辇去之 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'He ordered the servants to cut it into hundreds of pieces and remove it.'
  • 凡运致十八九担而后尽 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'It took transporting eighteen or nineteen loads to finish.'
  • 时人谓张子凶于祅祟 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'People at the time said that Zhang's son was ominous due to the supernatural.'
  • 戯目其堂为太嵗堂 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'They jokingly called the hall the Tai Sui Hall.'
  • 然亦亡恙 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'However, there was no harm.'
  • 后遭罹兵祸始荡为丘墟 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'Later, when they suffered from a military disaster, it became a ruin.'
  • 王顺伯祖母南剑夫人正张氏女云 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'Lady Nanjian, the grandmother of Wang Shunbo, was a daughter of the Zhang family.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 黄师宪 - Historical figure who took the provincial examination.
  • 陈应求 - Fellow villager of Huang Shixian.
  • 陈俊卿 - Historical figure who was a successful scholar-official.
  • 陈防 - Historical figure who was a fellow candidate.
  • 绍兴戊午 - Year 1158 AD, during the Southern Song Dynasty.
  • 莆田 - City in Fujian Province.
  • 省试 - Provincial examination, a crucial step in the imperial examination system.
  • 建安 - City in Fujian Province.
  • 梨山 - Specific location where Huang Shixian prayed.
  • 李侯庙 - Temple dedicated to a deity named Li Hou.
  • 姑苏 - City in Jiangsu Province.
  • 张比部 - Historical figure who was a deputy minister.
  • 崇宁 - Reign title of Emperor Huizong of Song, 1100-1110 AD.
  • 二浙 - Two Zhe regions, referring to Zhejiang Province.
  • 太嵗 - Term in Chinese astrology and feng shui.
  • 王顺伯 - Historical figure whose grandmother was a member of the Zhang family.
  • 南剑夫人 - Historical figure who was a daughter of the Zhang family.

  • 扼其颈 - Grasped its neck.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • The three public officials refer to the highest-ranking officials in ancient Chinese government, typically the Grand Tutor, Grand Works Minister, and Grand Marshal.

  • Yue Fei (岳家军) was a famous general during the Song Dynasty, leading the military forces known as Yue Fei's army.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 俄自携具就胡饮 - 俄 means soon or in a short while, 携具 means to bring utensils, 就胡饮 means to join the Hu people in drinking; describes a sudden action of joining the Hu people in a drinking session.
  • 从容白曰 - 从容 means calmly or composedly, 白曰 means to say; indicates a composed manner of speaking.
  • 我等尽力于君亦不少 - 我等 means we, 尽力 means to exert effort, 于君 means for you, 亦不少 means also not little; suggests that the speaker and others have made significant efforts for the listener.
  • 求此宅为庙 - 求 means to request, 此宅 means this house, 为庙 means to make it a temple; indicates a request to convert the house into a temple.
  • 庶几人神不相淆杂 - 庶几 means perhaps or hopefully, 人神 means humans and spirits, 不相淆杂 means not to be confused or mixed; expresses a hope that humans and spirits will not be confused or mixed.
  • 君却于比近别筑第 - 君 means you, 却 means but or however, 于比近 means in the vicinity, 别筑第 means to build a separate residence; suggests that the listener should build a separate residence in the nearby area.
  • 但用吾日前所饷足以办集 - 但 means only or just, 用 means to use, 吾日前所饷 means what I provided before, 足以办集 means enough to handle; indicates that what was provided earlier is sufficient to handle the matter.
  • 幸毋见拒 - 幸 means please, 毋 means do not, 见拒 means reject; is a polite request not to be rejected.
  • 此吾三世所居 - 此 means this, 吾 means my, 三世 means three generations, 所居 means dwelling; indicates that the place has been the dwelling of three generations.
  • 讵可轻议 - 讵 means how or how could, 可轻议 means be lightly discussed; suggests that the place cannot be lightly discussed or changed.
  • 择山冈好处奉为营一祠 - 择 means to choose, 山冈好处 means a good place on a hill, 奉为 means to set up as, 营一祠 means to build a shrine; indicates the intention to build a shrine in a good place on a hill.
  • 且任香火之责 - 且 means and, 任 means to take on, 香火 means incense and fire (symbolizing worship), 之责 means the responsibility; suggests taking on the responsibility of maintaining the shrine.
  • 皆奋言不可出语益悖 - 皆 means all, 奋言 means to speak vehemently, 不可 means cannot, 出语 means to speak out, 益悖 means increasingly contrary; indicates that all spoke vehemently, saying it cannot be done, and their words became increasingly contrary.
  • 自是遂造祟怪 - 自是 means from then on, 遂 means then, 造 means to create, 祟怪 means supernatural disturbances; indicates that from then on, supernatural disturbances occurred.
  • 胡不能堪谋于姻旧 - 胡 means he, 不能堪 means could not bear, 谋于 means to consult with, 姻旧 means relatives and old friends; indicates that he could not bear it and consulted with his relatives and old friends.
  • 将呼道士施法方归 - 将 means to intend, 呼 means to call, 道士 means Taoist priest, 施法 means to perform rituals, 方归 means just returned; indicates the intention to call a Taoist priest to perform rituals before returning.
  • 及门五人当道遮立 - 及门 means at the gate, 五人 means five people, 当道 means blocking the road, 遮立 means standing in the way; describes five people blocking the road at the gate.
  • 闻欲招法师见治吾乃正神享国家血食 - 闻 means heard, 欲招法师 means to invite a priest, 见治 means to treat, 吾乃正神 means I am a righteous spirit, 享国家血食 means enjoying sacrifices from the state; indicates that the spirits heard the intention to invite a priest to treat them, but they are righteous spirits who enjoy state sacrifices.
  • 只欲宅屋建庙未为大过 - 只欲 means only want, 宅屋 means house, 建庙 means to build a temple, 未为大过 means not a great fault; suggests that they only want the house to be converted into a temple, which is not a great fault.
  • 法师何为者哉 - 法师 means priest, 何为者 means what are you doing, 哉 is an interrogative particle; is a question asking what the priest is doing.
  • 虽汉天师复出吾亦不畏 - 虽 means even if, 汉天师 means the Heavenly Master of Han, 复出 means to reappear, 吾亦不畏 means I also do not fear; indicates that even if the Heavenly Master of Han reappeared, they would not fear.
  • 胡益以愁挠而扰惑日甚 - 胡 means he, 益 means increasingly, 以愁挠 means with worry and disturbance, 而扰惑 means and confusion, 日甚 means daily increasing; indicates that he became increasingly worried, disturbed, and confused daily.
  • 他日入市值道人行乞 - 他日 means another day, 入市 means to enter the market, 值 means to encounter, 道人 means Taoist, 行乞 means to beg; describes encountering a Taoist who was begging in the market.
  • 君面有忧色必遭鬼物所恼 - 君 means you, 面有忧色 means having a worried expression, 必 means certainly, 遭 means to suffer, 鬼物 means ghosts, 所恼 means troubled; indicates that the Taoist said the listener certainly suffered from ghosts troubling them.
  • 可从此进傥逢一小僧便祈之定能相救 - 可 means can, 从此进 means proceed from here, 傥 means if, 逢 means to meet, 一小僧 means a small monk, 便祈之 means pray to him, 定能相救 means definitely can save; suggests proceeding from there and praying to a small monk if encountered, who can definitely save them.
  • 胡惊谢方拟扣其详忽不见行至田间果遇僧即致恳祷 - 胡惊谢 means he was surprised and thanked, 方拟扣其详 means intended to ask for details, 忽不见 means suddenly disappeared, 行至田间 means walked to the fields, 果遇僧 means indeed met a monk, 即致恳祷 means immediately offered earnest prayers; describes the listener being surprised and thanking the Taoist, intending to ask for details, but the Taoist suddenly disappeared. The listener then walked to the fields and indeed met a monk, to whom he immediately offered earnest prayers.
  • 僧曰兹小事耳君姑归 - 僧曰 means the monk said, 兹小事耳 means this is a small matter, 君姑归 means you may return; indicates the monk's response that it is a small matter and the listener may return.

  • 眞武生辰 - The birthday of Zhenwu, a Taoist deity associated with martial arts and water.
  • 阖郭共建黄箓醮 - '阖郭' means the entire city or region, '共建' means to jointly build or organize, and '黄箓醮' refers to a Taoist ritual for warding off disasters and seeking blessings.
  • 供具甚整肃 - '供具' refers to the offerings or preparations made for the ritual, and '整肃' means orderly and solemn.
  • 受雇佣书人凭旧奏章 - '受雇佣书人' refers to the person hired to write the ritual text, and '凭旧奏章' means relying on the old ritual text as a reference.
  • 拱手相向若被束缚者 - '拱手相向' means to bow hands in greeting, and '若被束缚者' suggests a sense of being bound or constrained.
  • 三天门下不肯受接 - '三天' refers to the Three Pure Ones in Taoism, and '不肯受接' means they will not accept the offering.
  • 冒犯清律罪有所归 - '冒犯清律' means to violate the pure laws, and '罪有所归' suggests that the sin has a specific consequence.
  • 做一道理 - '做一道理' means to make a resolution or decision.
  • 奏过已得旨放罪 - '奏过' means to report to the higher authority, and '已得旨放罪' means the higher authority has granted forgiveness.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 寘乙于选中 - Literal meaning 'place 乙 in the selection'; refers to placing someone in a chosen position or category.
  • 累夕不怿 - Literal meaning 'restless for several nights'; indicates a state of worry or dissatisfaction.
  • 再读乙对策 - Literal meaning 'read 乙's response again'; refers to re-reading someone's written response or answer.
  • 风流之所靡 - Refers to the influence of fashionable or popular trends.
  • 习俗之所咻 - Refers to the influence of customs or traditions.
  • 喟然而作 - Literal meaning 'sighed and wrote'; indicates a sigh followed by writing.
  • 用齐人楚大夫子之事 - Refers to a specific historical or literary reference, possibly from the *Zuo Zhuan* or another classical text.
  • 正音 - Refers to the correct pronunciation or interpretation.
  • 犯庙讳 - Literal meaning 'violate the taboo of the temple'; refers to breaking a sacred or official taboo.

  • 幸而堕平地延颈仰望见五骑相逐来皆下马呼超曰如何攧在墈下 - Describes the aftermath of the dream: 'Fortunately, he fell on flat ground, stretched his neck to look up, and saw five riders chasing each other, all dismounting and calling out to him, 'How did you fall here?''

  • 不生怨恨 - Means 'no resentment or grudge,' expressing a lack of anger or bitterness.
  • 见已身卧床上 - Means 'seeing oneself lying on the bed,' a vivid description of the state of being close to death.
  • 指之曰 - Means 'pointing and saying,' indicating a gesture followed by a statement.
  • 早与他尽命 - Means 'let him die quickly,' expressing a desire for a swift end to suffering.
  • 莫教受苦 - Means 'do not let him suffer,' expressing a wish to avoid pain.
  • 黄衫曰 - Indicates the speaker is dressed in yellow robes, likely a reference to an official or a figure of authority.
  • 隂司取人不如此 - Means 'the underworld does not take people this way,' suggesting that the method of taking souls is different from what is described.
  • 只是引将去 - Means 'only to lead them away,' indicating a process of guiding the soul to the afterlife.
  • 如便与过了性命是违犯天条也 - Means 'if we were to end their life prematurely, it would violate the heavenly laws,' emphasizing the importance of following divine rules.
  • 驻留食顷 - Means 'staying for a meal's time,' indicating a brief pause.
  • 引手撮其喉 - Means 'reaching out and grasping the throat,' a vivid description of the act of leading the soul.
  • 觉如火中取出新锻铁器淬于水盆之声 - Means 'feeling like pulling a newly forged iron piece from fire and quenching it in a water basin,' a metaphor for the intensity of the experience.
  • 持索缚超 - Means 'holding a rope to bind the person,' indicating a method of restraint.
  • 不须尔 - Means 'no need for that,' expressing a dismissal of unnecessary actions.
  • 我决不窜走天涯海角也 - Means 'I will not run away to the ends of the earth,' indicating a commitment to stay.
  • 随使者去 - Means 'follow the messenger,' indicating compliance with the request.
  • 于道理合如此 - Means 'it is right according to reason,' expressing agreement with the logic of the situation.
  • 俄抵岳下第八司 - Means 'soon arriving at the eighth office under the mountain,' indicating a specific location in the afterlife.
  • 入至殿廷 - Means 'entering the hall,' describing the scene of entering a grand space.
  • 唱云 - Means 'singing out,' indicating a formal announcement or call.

  • 初离家时躯干骤长大如寺门金刚 - Means 'when he first left home, his body suddenly grew large like a guardian deity at the temple gate,' describing a physical transformation.
  • 自骇其异 - Means 'startled by his own strangeness,' indicating a reaction to the unusual change.
  • 至是缩小才如茶托 - Means 'by then, he had shrunk to the size of a tea saucer,' describing the reversal of the transformation.
  • 主者问汝在阳间看诵是何经典 - Means 'the judge asks what scriptures you read in the mortal world,' indicating a question about religious practice.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一官人來言身是黄教授今當為爾子 - Classical narrative opening where a minor official introduces himself as Huang Jiaoshi (a professor) and states that he will be the child's guardian.
  • 兹覽來説彼此冥符其必有嘉證 - Phrase indicating that the speaker has reviewed the discussion and found a mysterious coincidence, suggesting a good omen.
  • 楷又申恳备至祈以诞子时切相报 - Phrase where the person named Kai (楷) expresses deep gratitude and requests immediate notification of the birth of the child.
  • 遂还逮十二月二十一日平旦陈妇生男五君名之曰万顷字之曰梦应以显厥祥 - Detailed account of the birth of a male child on December 21st at dawn, named Wanqing and given the courtesy name Mengying, to signify the auspicious sign.
  • 且驰书语楷楷亟往视之儿犹未满月望楷入室迎面而笑 - Sequence describing how a letter was sent to Kai, who promptly visited the newborn, and the child greeted him with a smile.
  • 及长读书有声淳熈甲午预乡荐然蹭蹬二十年绍熈癸丑始擢第调兴化尉 - Biographical summary of the child's life, noting his success in the imperial exams and his eventual appointment as a magistrate in Xinghua.
  • 其弟大猷书本末以示人 - Statement indicating that the child's younger brother, Dàyóu, wrote a detailed account of the events and shared it with others.
  • 鼷防蚁虎 - Metaphorical expression comparing two small but significant creatures, the mole-rat and the ant-tiger.
  • 鼷防为郊牛孽书于春秋后来书鲜或纪载 - Historical reference to the mole-rat's appearance in the *Spring and Autumn Annals* and its rarity in later records.
  • 而十年以来吾乡忽有之姓孙份家一黄牯在栏不食水草但定立不动 - Description of a recent occurrence where a yellow ox in a family named Sun refused to eat or move.

  • 得钱七十千 - Means 'received seventy thousand coins.'
  • 在籍嵗余矣 - Means 'been registered for over a year.'
  • 语竟不觉堕泪 - Means 'after speaking, one could not help but shed tears.'
  • 一坐倾骇 - Means 'everyone present was astonished.'
  • 争致问 - Means 'rushed to ask questions.'
  • 东老曰其话甚长 - Means 'Dong Laoshi said that the story is very long.'
  • 兹未可以立谈尽 - Means 'it cannot be fully explained in a short conversation.'
  • 他日当言之 - Means 'I will tell you another day.'
  • 酒罢归馆舎 - Means 'after the wine feast, returned to the inn.'

  • 其人于震亨为表妹 - Means 'the person is a cousin of Zhenheng.'
  • 必嫁之 - Means 'must be married.'
  • 当以此行所得诸防及诸郡饷赆为资送费 - Means 'should use the money obtained from this trip and the gifts from various prefectures as wedding expenses.'
  • 今且托之于令人所 - Means 'for now, entrust it to someone.'
  • 天下义事岂应一人独擅 - Means 'a matter of righteousness should not be monopolized by one person.'
  • 吾当以二十万钱助之 - Means 'I will contribute twenty thousand coins to help.'
  • 遂往成都 - Means 'then went to Chengdu.'
  • 越一月复还 - Means 'after a month, returned.'
  • 合所得为五十万 - Means 'the total amount received was fifty thousand coins.'
  • 悉付备 - Means 'all handed over to prepare.'
  • 喜曰已为择一佳壻 - Means 'said happily that a good husband has been chosen.'
  • 即嫁之矣 - Means 'and will be married immediately.'
  • 姓史失其名 - Means 'his surname is Shi, but his name is unknown.'
  • 次年预乡荐 - Means 'the following year, he was recommended for the local examination.'
  • 物色其兄弟所在 - Means 'searched for the whereabouts of his brothers.'

  • 汉州之后赖以不绝 - Means 'after Han Prefecture, they relied on this to survive.'

  • 吴守 - Refers to Governor Wu, a high-ranking official.
  • 东老 - Refers to Dong Laoshi, a respected elder or teacher.
  • 薛媪 - Refers to an old woman named Xue, likely a brothel owner or trafficker.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The long sentence '我姑也吾闻汝母子流落寻觅每年不谓邂逅于此又厯道所从来乃知昨为继母鬻于薛媪得钱七十千今在籍嵗余矣' can be broken down into: 'I heard that your mother and you have been wandering and searching every year, not expecting to meet here unexpectedly. After tracing the path you came from, I learned that your stepmother sold you to an old woman named Xue for seventy thousand coins, and you have been registered for over a year.'
  • The sentence '语竟不觉堕泪一坐倾骇争致问东老曰其话甚长兹未可以立谈尽他日当言之' can be broken down into: 'After speaking, one could not help but shed tears. Everyone present was astonished and rushed to ask questions. Dong Laoshi said that the story is very long and cannot be fully explained in a short conversation. I will tell you another day.'
  • The sentence '酒罢归馆舎翌日情偕行母来吴守亦至因备述本末丐为除籍' can be broken down into: 'After the wine feast, returned to the inn. The next day, accompanied by your mother, came together. Governor Wu also arrived. Then recounted the beginning and end and asked to be removed from the registry.'
  • The sentence '天下义事岂应一人独擅吾当以二十万钱助之' can be broken down into: 'A matter of righteousness should not be monopolized by one person. I will contribute twenty thousand coins to help.'
  • The sentence '合所得为五十万悉付备喜曰已为择一佳壻即嫁之矣' can be broken down into: 'The total amount received was fifty thousand coins. All handed over to prepare. Said happily that a good husband has been chosen and will be married immediately.'

  • 持归家因此生计浸丰不假经营而钱自至越两嵗如天运鬼输盈塞败屋几满室万缗 - Describes how the family's financial situation improved significantly after acquiring the mirror, with wealth accumulating naturally without effort.
